{"step_by_step_thinking": "Dupilumab is indicated for the treatment of moderate-to-severe asthma, particularly in patients with eosinophilic or type 2 inflammation. Multiple documents confirm its efficacy: Document [1] states that dupilumab enhances lung function and reduces severe exacerbations in uncontrolled persistent asthma when used with inhaled corticosteroids and LABA. Document [9] reports a 60% to 80% reduction in exacerbations in patients on ICS-LABA. Document [22] confirms FDA approval for add-on maintenance treatment in pediatric patients aged 6 and older with oral corticosteroid dependence or eosinophilic phenotype. Document [15] explicitly states that dupilumab is used as a maintenance treatment for asthma in adults and children. Although it is not approved for treating acute bronchospasm or status asthmaticus, it is effective as an add-on therapy for managing chronic asthma. The evidence from multiple sources, including FDA-approved indications and clinical studies, consistently supports its effectiveness in reducing exacerbations and improving asthma control. Therefore, the answer is clearly 'yes'.", "answer_choice": "A"}
